name: Metamorphic Testing

x.defectClass: Bugs in programs with no easy oracle, found via violated relations between related inputs/outputs.
x.oracle: Metamorphic relations — invariants linking the outputs of related inputs (e.g. sin(x)=sin(π−x); sorting a permuted list gives the same multiset).
x.automationLevel: High once relations are defined — transform inputs and check the relation automatically.
blindSpots: Bugs that satisfy all chosen relations escape
Quality depends entirely on the relations devised
Does not pin absolute correctness, only relative
x.cost: Medium — designing good metamorphic relations is the effort.
x.lifecycleStage: Development / testing ML, scientific, and oracle-poor software.

def: Testing software that lacks a clear oracle by checking that transforming an input changes the output in a predictable, required way (a metamorphic relation).
problem: For many programs (ML models, scientific code, search) you cannot state the exact correct output, so example-based assertions are impossible.
mechanism: Identify metamorphic relations — properties linking the outputs of related inputs (e.g. scaling all features should not change a classifier’s label; reversing a route gives the same distance). Generate input pairs and assert the relation holds.
benefits: Tests programs with no explicit oracle
limits: Only as good as the relations chosen
failures: Weak relations that any buggy program satisfies
whenNot: A precise oracle is available (assert it directly)
myths: "Untestable software exists" — metamorphic relations test many oracle-free programs
